title: Annual input data
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
The adopted CMP specification must replace this template with exact series and rules. Until then, this page defines the minimum metadata required for each candidate test and annual dry run.

title: Annual input data
text:
1 Validation classes

Schema: expected columns, types, names, years, and uniqueness.
Units: expected magnitude and explicit conversion.
Completeness: required years and indices are present.
Plausibility: changes outside documented bounds are flagged, not automatically corrected.
Vintage: the source date and revision state are recorded.
Identity: the archived file checksum matches the calculation record.

Missing or revised data must follow the adopted specification. Analysts should not invent a replacement rule during the annual calculation.

title: Annual CMP application
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
This workflow describes how an adopted CMP should be applied as new data become available. Candidate dry runs should follow the same controls.
flowchart TD
  A["Freeze CMP version and code commit"] --> B["Receive and archive annual data"]
  B --> C["Validate schema, units, completeness, and vintage"]
  C -->|fail| X["Stop and document exception"]
  C -->|pass| D["Calculate estimator and status metric"]
  D --> E["Apply HCR"]
  E --> F["Apply interannual and implementation constraints"]
  F --> G["Run independent or automated cross-check"]
  G -->|mismatch| X
  G -->|match| H["Review exceptional-circumstances indicators"]
  H --> I["Publish advice and complete calculation record"]

title: Annual CMP application
text:
1 1. Freeze the governing specification
Record the CMP identifier and version, repository commit, software environment, management year, analyst, reviewer, and decision record authorizing use.

title: Annual CMP application
text:
2 2. Archive inputs
Store the received files unchanged. Record provider, receipt time, data vintage, and checksums. Create a separate validated copy only through a scripted, reviewable transformation.

title: Annual CMP application
text:
3 3. Validate before calculation
Apply the input data contract. A failed required check stops the routine calculation. The failure and any subsequent authorized treatment become part of the permanent annual record.

title: Annual CMP application
text:
4 4. Calculate transparent intermediates
Save the transformed index values, reference-period values, combined metric, raw HCR recommendation, each constraint adjustment, allocation, rounding step, and final advice. Do not save only the final number.

title: Annual CMP application
text:
5 5. Cross-check
Compare against a frozen worked example and, where feasible, an independent implementation. Differences outside the specification’s numerical tolerance must be resolved before advice is released.

title: Annual CMP application
text:
6 6. Exceptional circumstances
Run only the indicators and response process defined in the adopted specification. An exceptional-circumstances review may trigger additional scientific or management consideration; it must not silently rewrite the CMP.

title: Annual CMP application
text:
7 7. Publish the calculation record
The record should contain inputs and checksums, validation results, intermediate calculations, final advice, software versions, warnings, analyst and reviewer sign-off, and links to any exception decision.

title: MSE workflow
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
This page condenses SCW17/Paper-04. It locates the documentation site within the broader analytical system without duplicating that system.
flowchart LR
  J["JJM assessment configurations"] --> C["Condition operating models"]
  C --> O["OM / OEM / IEM bundles"]
  O --> M["Apply CMPs in jmMSE"]
  M --> P["Performance metrics and diagnostics"]
  P --> D["CMP comparison and decision"]
  D --> S["Versioned specification in jmMSE26"]
  S --> Y["Annual operational application"]
  Y --> V["Archived inputs, calculations, and advice"]

title: MSE workflow
text:
1 Separation of responsibilities
jmMSE26 must not become an independent fork of the MSE engine. Executable snippets here are conformance examples or thin wrappers pinned to a jmMSE commit. Operating models, recruitment assumptions, movement assumptions, reference points, and performance metrics remain governed by their scientific source repositories.

title: MSE workflow
text:
2 Reproducibility chain
Every performance claim should identify the OM set, CMP version, random-seed policy, iteration count, projection period, metric definition, output artifact, and source commit. Every operational application should identify its input data vintage and the adopted CMP version.

title: CMP evidence and development history
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

title: CMP evidence and development history
text:
1 Current candidate evidence from jmMSE
The current merged repository provides three mutually supporting signals:

model_tune.R defines and tunes MP29 and MP32 using the same five-index empirical estimator, minimum catch, limit, and annual constraints.
The 25 July 2026 presentation identifies MP29 as the optimal hockeystick run, MP32 as the optimal powerramp run, and MP29 as a potentially good candidate after their direct trade-off comparison.
model_robustness.R explicitly selects tun29 and tun32 for application across the robustness OM set.

The presentation also reports 500-iteration versions, MP41 and MP42, with slightly different retuned triggers. These are treated here as replication sensitivity checks rather than new operational candidates.
Numerical results should be regenerated or linked to immutable artifacts before they support a formal selection decision.

title: CMP evidence and development history
CMP evidence and development history
CMP evidence and development history

Yes

Desirable

title: MP29 hockeystick candidate
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

title: MP29 hockeystick candidate
text:
1 Identity







Component
Current definition




jmMSE run
tun29, labelled MP29


Estimator
cpues.ind


Indices
Chile_AcousN, Chile_CPUE, Peru_Artis, Peru_Ind, Offshore_CPUE


Reference years
2019-2023


Recent-data window
3 years


Combination and metric
combine = "mean", metric = "mean"


HCR
hockeystick.hcr; hockeystick.hcr_rob in multi-stock robustness runs


Output
Catch


Implementation
split.is, using last-five-year catch proportions

title: MP29 hockeystick candidate
text:
2 HCR parameters



Parameter
Value




Minimum catch, min
270


Limit metric, lim
0.1


Trigger metric
2.12


Target catch
2000


Initial catch for constraints
1385


Maximum annual decrease multiplier, dlow
0.85


Maximum annual increase multiplier, dupp
1.20



The trigger is the tuned value reported in the July presentation. Before formal use it should be read from a frozen control object or normative registry, not reconstructed by rerunning the tuner.

title: MP29 hockeystick candidate
text:
3 Rule
Let \(I_t\) be the annual mean metric returned by the specified estimator. The unconstrained catch recommendation \(C_t^*\) is
\[
C_t^* =
\begin{cases}
270, & I_t \le 0.1,\\
270 + (I_t-0.1)\dfrac{2000-270}{2.12-0.1}, & 0.1 < I_t < 2.12,\\
2000, & I_t \ge 2.12.
\end{cases}
\]
The interannual constraint limits the recommendation to 85%-120% of the previous recommendation. In the first application year the current code uses 1385 as the previous value.

title: MP29 hockeystick candidate
text:
4 Evidence status
The July presentation identifies MP29 as the optimal hockeystick minimum/limit run and reports lower IACC than MP32, with slightly higher short-term catches. model_robustness.R advances MP29 alongside MP32 across robustness OMs. Final adoption still requires complete, reproducible robustness results and an approved operational data contract.
Primary sources: model_tune.R, model_robustness.R, and the July presentation source.

title: MP29 hockeystick candidate
section: 5 Annual application requirements still to resolve

Reference years
2019-2023


Recent-data window
3 years


Combination and metric

HCR

Catch

title: MP32 powerramp candidate
text:
2 HCR parameters



Parameter
Value




Minimum catch, min
270


Limit metric, lim
0.1


Trigger metric
1.42


Target catch
1500


Initial catch for constraints
1385


Maximum annual decrease multiplier, dlow
0.85


Maximum annual increase multiplier, dupp
1.20

title: MP32 powerramp candidate
text:
3 Rule
Let \(I_t\) be the annual estimator metric and
\[
x_t = \frac{I_t-0.1}{1.42-0.1}.
\]
The current jmMSE powerramp.hcr calculates the unconstrained recommendation as
\[
C_t^* =
\begin{cases}
270, & I_t \le 0.1,\\
270 + (1500-270)x_t^{2.5}, & 0.1 < I_t < 1.42,\\
1500(0.2+0.8x_t)^{0.6}, & I_t \ge 1.42.
\end{cases}
\]
Unlike a capped hockeystick, the upper branch continues to rise above the trigger. The annual constraint subsequently limits the result to 85%-120% of the previous recommendation; the first-year reference is 1385.

title: MP32 powerramp candidate
text:
4 Evidence status
The July presentation identifies MP32 as the optimal powerramp minimum/limit run. The MP29-MP32 comparison reports broadly similar long-term catch and stock performance, with MP32 showing higher IACC than MP29. model_robustness.R advances it alongside MP29.
Primary sources: model_tune.R, model_robustness.R, and the July presentation source.

title: MP32 powerramp candidate
section: 5 Annual application requirements still to resolve

title: CMP specification template
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
The Normative CMP Specification Template defines the information required to turn a candidate management procedure into an unambiguous and reproducible management rule. Once completed and approved, the normative specification governs implementation: it identifies the authorized inputs, calculations, parameter values, constraints, exceptional-circumstances provisions, and code version. Background explanations and MSE performance results support selection of the CMP but do not alter its operational definition. Where explanatory text and the approved normative specification differ, the normative specification takes precedence.
Copy this structure for every shortlisted CMP. Text labelled normative governs implementation; explanatory evidence belongs in the evidence section.

title: CMP specification template
text:
1 Identity and authority



Field
Required value




CMP identifier
Stable identifier; never reused


Version
Semantic or dated version


Status
Test, shortlisted, recommended, adopted, or retired


Effective period
First and last management year, if applicable


Decision record
Body, meeting, document, date, and paragraph


Code reference
Repository, commit, file, and function/control name

title: CMP specification template
text:
2 Normative inputs
For every input state the owner, series name, unit, spatial coverage, seasonal coverage, annual cutoff date, revision policy, missing-value rule, and transformation. Specify exactly which data vintages are eligible.

title: CMP specification template
CMP specification template
text:
4 Worked example
Provide frozen input data, intermediate values, final advice, and a checksum or automated regression test. A reviewer must be able to reproduce the published answer without access to mutable external data.

title: CMP specification template
text:
5 Evidence and limitations
Link to MSE runs, operating models, performance metrics, diagnostics, and known limitations. Evidence explains selection but does not redefine the CMP.

title: CMP specification template
text:
6 Change history
Every change must state whether it is editorial, implementation-equivalent, or scientifically material. Scientifically material changes require renewed MSE evaluation unless an approving decision explicitly states otherwise.

title: CMP registry
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

title: CMP registry
text:
1 Status vocabulary







Status
Meaning




Test candidate
Defined sufficiently for testing; not shortlisted or adopted.


Advanced candidate
Selected in the current analytical workflow for robustness testing; formal status not yet confirmed.


Shortlisted
Retained by the working group for formal comparative evaluation.


Recommended
Supported by a documented working-group recommendation.


Adopted
Formally adopted through the applicable SPRFMO process.


Retired
Preserved for traceability but no longer under active consideration.



The primary registry is derived from the merged jmMSE main branch. model_robustness.R selects tun29 and tun32 for robustness evaluation, and the 25 July 2026 presentation describes MP29 as a potentially good candidate and MP32 as the optimal powerramp run. They are advanced candidates, not adopted procedures.




Table 1: Current advanced candidates derived from jmMSE. Tuned trigger values are those reported in the 25 July 2026 presentation.

title: CMP registry
text:
2 Candidate pages

MP29: empirical all-index estimator plus hockeystick HCR.
MP32: empirical all-index estimator plus power-ramp HCR.

title: CMP registry
text:
3 Development lineage
The jmMSE tuning sequence tested targets, minimum catches, limit values, HCR families, and estimator variants.








Run
Role in development
July presentation interpretation




MP16
Hockeystick, target 2000, no minimum/limit
Potential candidate before minimum/limit refinement


MP25
Powerramp, target 1500, no minimum/limit
Potential candidate before minimum/limit refinement


MP29
Hockeystick with minimum 270 and limit 0.1
Optimal hockeystick run; advanced to robustness tests


MP32
Powerramp with minimum 270 and limit 0.1
Optimal powerramp run; advanced to robustness tests


MP35-40
CPUE-only, shortcut-assessment, and perfect-information variants
Estimator sensitivity and diagnostic comparisons


MP41-42
500-iteration versions of the MP29/MP32 designs
Replication-count sensitivity, not distinct operational CMPs



The earlier SCW17/Paper-09 rows are retained in source history as workshop test proposals. They are not the primary candidate list for this site.

title: CMP registry
text:
4 Interpretation safeguards

min, trigger, target, and initial are catch quantities in the current code and presentation; final normative units must be stated before adoption.
dlow and dupp are interannual lower and upper multipliers where supplied.
Index names match model_tune.R exactly.
The trigger was tuned to a 0.60 probability for SB0green over 2041-2050 on the reference setup. It is part of the candidate version and should not be silently retuned during annual application.

title: CMP registry
text:
5 Promotion requirement
A candidate can move beyond test status only when it has a completed specification, reproducible performance evidence, an implementation test using frozen inputs, and a decision record identifying the approving body and date.

title: Management-procedure components
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
This page reorganizes the architecture and inventory in SCW17/Paper-03. In jmMSE, a management procedure normally combines three controlled modules:
flowchart LR
  D["Observed annual data"] --> E["Estimator: est"]
  E --> S["Status or index metric"]
  S --> H["Harvest control rule: hcr"]
  H --> R["Catch recommendation"]
  R --> I["Implementation system: isys"]
  I --> A["Implemented catch by fishery or stock"]

title: Management-procedure components
section: 1 Families represented in the SCW17 inventory
text:
1 Families represented in the SCW17 inventory









Family
Typical estimator
Typical HCR
Role at SCW17




Fixed catch reference
Perfect information
Fixed catch
Diagnostic baseline


Shortcut assessment
Depletion estimate
Hockeystick or buffer
Assessment-based comparison


Empirical index
CPUE/index aggregate
Buffer or hockeystick
Main CMP development family


SPiCT assessment
SPiCT biomass/status
Buffer
Model-based candidate


Multi-index/meta
Multiple index scores
Meta-HCR
Exploratory candidate


Banking/borrowing
Existing estimator/HCR
Existing HCR plus modified isys
Implementation sensitivity



These families are an inventory, not a decision about which procedure should be selected. Exact CMP identity requires the module versions, full argument set, input definitions, and implementation rules recorded together.

title: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
crumbs: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

title: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
text:
Purpose
This site is the durable specification and implementation guide for candidate management procedures (CMPs) evaluated for South Pacific jack mackerel. It is derived primarily from the current jmMSE repository and brings together:

the MP29 and MP32 candidate definitions advanced into robustness testing;
the end-to-end MSE workflow;
evidence from tuning, trade-off, and robustness comparisons; and
instructions for applying a selected procedure when new annual data become available.

SCW17 Papers 03, 04, 08, and 09 provide historical context and documentation structure, but they do not define the current candidate registry.
The scientific simulation engine remains in SPRFMO/jmMSE. This repository records what a CMP is, why it remains under consideration, how it is applied, and which version of code and evidence supports it.
crumbs: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

title: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
text:
Start here

CMP registry: the two candidates currently advanced by jmMSE, MP29 and MP32.
MP29 and MP32: candidate-specific rules and annual calculation details.
Annual application: controlled operational sequence from new data to advice.
Slick explorer: validated conversion of MP29/MP32 performance outputs for interactive review.
MSE workflow: where CMPs fit within the assessment and simulation system.
Evidence and history: current jmMSE evidence with SCW17 development context.
Traceability: source commits, review state, and change control.
crumbs: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

title: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
text:
Governing principle
The normative CMP specification is separate from explanatory text and MSE results. A scientific result may change the status of a candidate, but it does not silently change the estimator, HCR, implementation system, parameter values, or exceptional-circumstances rules.
crumbs: SPRFMO Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures

title: Traceability and change control
text:
1 Seed provenance








Source
Commit used for scaffold
Reorganized material




SPRFMO/jmMSE
94d4d325ba46220f56097b3de90824c83b7cf3cd
Primary source: MP29/MP32 controls, robustness selection, HCR implementation, and July presentation


SPRFMO/scw17
0eb97c9c0329f5ec3338e3cf885f6e1f184c4780
Secondary source: Papers 03, 04, 08, and 09 plus Quarto conventions



The current registry derives from jmMSE, not the SCW17 test table. It does not assert that MP29 or MP32 has been formally recommended or adopted.

title: Traceability and change control
Traceability and change control
text:
3 Change classes








Class
Examples
Required review




Editorial
Spelling, navigation, clearer non-normative explanation
Documentation review


Implementation-equivalent
Refactor proven to return identical results
Code review plus regression test


Scientific
Parameter, index, window, HCR, constraint, allocation, or fallback change
Renewed scientific review and normally MSE evaluation


Status
Test to shortlist, recommendation, adoption, or retirement
Cited decision record

title: Traceability and change control
text:
4 Open items at scaffold time

Confirm the formal working-group status and stable identifiers for MP29 and MP32.
Reconcile the 100-iteration MP29/MP32 tuned triggers with the 500-iteration MP41/MP42 sensitivity values before freezing a normative version.
Populate immutable evidence artifacts from agreed jmMSE runs.
Complete normative units, annual cutoffs, missing-data rules, rounding, and exceptional-circumstances provisions.
Add frozen worked examples and automated conformance tests.

title: Load candidates into Slick
text:
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation
    
    Jack Mackerel Candidate Management Procedures
The project-local adapter R/build_candidate_slick.R converts current jmMSE performance outputs into a validated Slick object. It does not run the MSE and does not fabricate missing results.

title: Load candidates into Slick
text:
1 Required input
Use either:

a long performance data.frame/data.table; or
an FLmses-like S4 object with a populated performance slot.

The input must contain MP29 and MP32 runs and the columns run, om, biol, statistic, year, iter, and data. At minimum, statistics SBMSY, FMSY, and C must be present. IACC is included when available.
The current checkout does not yet contain MP29/MP32 result artifacts in model/tune/ or model/robustness/; the available top-level tuning artifacts end at MP22. Run the current candidate/robustness workflow before attempting the production export.

title: Load candidates into Slick
text:
2 Build the Slick object
From the jmMSE26 repository root:


Show implementation detail
source("R/build_candidate_slick.R")

slick <- build_candidate_slick(
  performance_file = "../jmMSE/model/robustness/robs.rds",
  out_file = "output/jm_candidates.slick"
)


The builder creates:

MPs() metadata for MP29 and MP32 from the registry;
OMs() metadata for each OM/stock combination;
Timeseries() values ordered as iteration x OM x MP x indicator x year; and
Kobe() values for SB/SBMSY and F/FMSY over projection years.

It calls Check() on the time-series, Kobe, and final Slick objects, calls methods::validObject(), and writes the .slick file only after validation.

title: Load candidates into Slick
text:
3 Inspect or launch


Show implementation detail
library(Slick)

slick <- readRDS("output/jm_candidates.slick")
Check(slick)
methods::validObject(slick)

dim(Value(Timeseries(slick)))
Metadata(MPs(slick))
Design(slick)

App(slick = slick)

title: Load candidates into Slick
text:
4 Reproducibility record
Archive the .slick file together with the source performance artifact, its checksum, the jmMSE commit, the jmMSE26 commit, and the R package versions.
